config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. I do not think society should automatically take priority over the rights of the individual, because that can easily become an excuse for abuse by authorities or the majority. In my view, individual rights are the foundation of a fair society, and public interests should be pursued without trampling basic freedoms. In Indonesia, I have seen how important it is to balance community values with personal rights rather than letting one completely override the other.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus selalu didahulukan di atas hak individu, karena di Amerika saya melihat betapa mudahnya alasan “demi kepentingan umum” dipakai untuk membenarkan penyalahgunaan kekuasaan. Hak individu bukan penghalang bagi masyarakat; justru itu pagar agar mayoritas atau pemerintah tidak melangkahi kebebasan dasar orang. Kepentingan publik memang penting, tetapi harus dibatasi oleh hak-hak yang tidak boleh dikorbankan sembarangan, seperti kebebasan berpendapat, proses hukum yang adil, dan perlindungan dari diskriminasi. Kalau prioritasnya dibalik tanpa batas yang jelas, yang sering terjadi bukan masyarakat yang lebih baik, melainkan masyarakat yang lebih kuat menekan yang lemah.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should not automatically outrank the rights of the individual, because that is exactly how abuse by the state or the majority becomes normalized. From my perspective in Indonesia, I have seen that community harmony matters, but it cannot be used as a reason to silence dissent, restrict due process, or treat minority rights as optional. Public interest is important, yet it should be pursued within clear limits that protect basic freedoms, otherwise “for society” becomes a convenient excuse for injustice.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Kepentingan masyarakat tidak boleh otomatis mengalahkan hak individu, karena di Amerika saya melihat betapa cepatnya slogan “demi kebaikan bersama” dipakai untuk menekan kebebasan yang seharusnya dilindungi. Argumen tentang harmoni sosial memang penting, tetapi kalau dipakai tanpa batas, itu justru membuka jalan bagi mayoritas atau negara untuk membungkam orang yang tidak populer. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang sehat bukan yang paling mudah memaksa warganya patuh, melainkan yang paling kuat menjaga hak dasar bahkan saat itu tidak nyaman secara politik. Kepentingan publik harus dijalankan, tetapi hanya selama tidak merusak proses hukum, kebebasan berpendapat, dan perlindungan bagi kelompok minoritas.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should take priority over individual rights as a general rule, because in Indonesia that idea can too easily be used to justify pressure from the state, the majority, or local power holders. Community harmony is important, but it becomes harmful when it is used to silence criticism, weaken due process, or treat minority protections as negotiable. Public interests should matter, but they must be limited by clear rights that cannot be overridden whenever it is convenient. A fair society is not one that simply demands obedience in the name of the common good; it is one that protects people even when that protection is uncomfortable.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak keras gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus diprioritaskan di atas hak individu sebagai aturan umum. Dari perspektif saya di Amerika, alasan “demi kebaikan bersama” terlalu sering dipakai untuk membenarkan penindasan, terutama ketika mayoritas atau pemerintah merasa paling tahu apa yang terbaik. Kepentingan publik memang nyata dan penting, tetapi nilainya justru bergantung pada apakah ia dijalankan tanpa menghancurkan kebebasan berbicara, proses hukum, dan perlindungan minoritas. Masyarakat yang sehat bukan yang paling mudah memaksa orang patuh, melainkan yang paling disiplin membatasi kekuasaannya sendiri.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
